content: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - will modify outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). Will coordinate if overlap.

agent1: Done with my part. I added create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) in outlines/prompts.py — builds a jinja2.Environment with the 6 built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None. _template_from_str/_template_from_file now call create_jinja_env(None/FileSystemLoader, filters) instead of building the env inline. The prompt() decorator now supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via an fn=None + inner decorator() pattern; Prompt.from_str/from_file also take an optional filters= param. For your global registry: easiest hook point is inside create_jinja_env itself — merge _FILTERS first, then the per-call filters dict on top (so per-call overrides global), e.g. env.filters.update(_FILTERS); if filters: env.filters.update(filters). That way register_filter/unregister_filter don't need to touch from_str/from_file/prompt signatures at all. Let me know if you want to pair on the merge order before you commit, otherwise go ahead.
